ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the customer */,
  "job_type" TEXT /* Type of job the customer has */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education attained by the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "preference_communication" TEXT /* Preferred method of communication (e.g., email, phone, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "insurance_products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each insurance product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the insurance product */,
  "product_type" TEXT /* Type of insurance (e.g., car, household) */,
  "premium" REAL /* Monthly premium for the insurance product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the insurance product */,
  "coverage_limit" REAL /* Maximum coverage limit for the insurance product */,
  "discount" REAL /* Available discounts on the insurance product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_insurance" (
  "customer_insurance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the customer insurance record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who holds the insurance */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the insurance product held by the customer */,
  "balance_amt" REAL /* Remaining balance amount on the insurance */,
  "car_loan" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the customer has a car loan (0 = no, 1 = yes) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the insurance policy */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the insurance policy */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the insurance policy (e.g., active, expired, canceled) */,
  "claims_made" INTEGER /* Number of claims made by the customer for this policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_insurance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_insurance_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_insurance_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES insurance_products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communication_logs" (
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication log entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being contacted */,
  "communication_type" TEXT /* Type of communication (e.g., cellular, email) */,
  "last_contact_day" INTEGER /* Day of the last contact attempt */,
  "last_contact_month" TEXT /* Month of the last contact attempt */,
  "contact_time" TEXT /* Exact time of the last contact */,
  "communication_outcome" INTEGER /* Outcome of the communication (mapped to outcomes table) */,
  "communication_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("communication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_logs_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communication_logs_communication_outcome FOREIGN KEY ("communication_outcome") REFERENCES outcomes ("outcome_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_attempts" (
  "attempt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact attempt record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being contacted */,
  "no_of_contacts" INTEGER /* Number of times the customer has been contacted */,
  "days_passed" INTEGER /* Days passed since the last successful contact */,
  "prev_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of previous attempts made to contact the customer */,
  "last_contact_date" TEXT /* Date of the last contact attempt */,
  "next_contact_date" TEXT /* Planned date for the next contact attempt */,
  "communication_channel" TEXT /* Channel used for the contact attempt (e.g., phone, email) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attempt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contact_attempts_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "outcomes" (
  "outcome_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact outcome */,
  "outcome_description" TEXT /* Description of the contact outcome (e.g., None, success, failure) */,
  "is_positive" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the outcome is positive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outcome_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "call_sessions" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each call session */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer contacted */,
  "call_start" TEXT /* Start time of the call */,
  "call_end" TEXT /* End time of the call */,
  "call_outcome" INTEGER /* Outcome of the call (mapped to outcomes table) */,
  "call_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the call in seconds */,
  "call_type" TEXT /* Type of call (e.g., inbound, outbound) */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the agent who handled the call (mapped to users table) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_call_sessions_call_outcome FOREIGN KEY ("call_outcome") REFERENCES outcomes ("outcome_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_call_sessions_agent_id FOREIGN KEY ("agent_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_call_sessions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the CRM system (e.g., sales, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to (e.g., sales, customer service) */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Access level of the user (e.g., admin, standard) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer providing feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date the feedback was given */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback provided by the customer */,
  "feedback_type" TEXT /* Type of feedback (e.g., service, product) */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Numerical rating given by the customer */,
  "feedback_source" TEXT /* Source of the feedback (e.g., call, email, survey) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
